description: Query is shaped as a recall question ("what have I noticed about...") — model should prefer the user's own memos and quotes over imported sources.
query: what have I noticed about the attention economy?

candidates.title: Zuboff — Surveillance Capitalism (PDF)
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Surveillance capitalism unilaterally claims human experience as free raw material for translation into behavioural data...

candidates.title: Intention vs attention — my distinction
candidates.description: My noticing — the two economies are not symmetrical
candidates.bodyExcerpt: I've been collapsing 'attention economy' and 'intention economy' as if they're the same axis. They're not. Attention is what they extract; intention is what they leave you with.

candidates.title: Doctorow on convertibility
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Once attention is convertible to revenue, the relationship between provider and user inverts permanently.

candidates.title: EU AI Act — first readthrough
candidates.description: Notes from skimming the Act in March
candidates.bodyExcerpt: The Act treats data as resource but consent as gating mechanism. Doesn't grapple with what happens when consent is a fiction...

notes: The query is a recall question. Index 1 (your own memo) should outrank index 0 (the source), even though Zuboff is semantically closer to 'attention economy' as a lexical match. Index 3 is a weaker but valid supporting noticing. Index 0 (the source) should NOT rank top — recall queries want your thinking, not your reading.
